videos.description:
It’s not just pickleball, it’s team pickleball.
Coed lineups. City pride. Matches that come down to a final DreamBreaker™.

This is Major League Pickleball, where team chemistry is the difference maker and every event builds toward the hardest trophy to win in the sport. 

20 teams. The 2026 season takes place over 13 events this summer. Join us at an event or tune-in and catch your favorite team.

videos.title: 2026 MLP Draft presented by Skechers
videos.description:
Tune in starting at 10am ET as the 2026 MLP Draft presented by Skechers unfolds live. Dave Fleming and Will Daughton are breaking down the first 28 picks as the first two male and two female roster slots are filled for each team. Expect real-time reactions, insights, and a front row seat to understanding teams' selections.
⁠
The remaining 38 selections and complete rosters will be posted in real-t

videos.title: Inside MLP - 2026 Draft Rules and Preview
videos.description:
In this edition of Inside MLP, Will Daughton explains the new rules and parts of the 2026 MLP Draft, previews every team's pre-draft roster, and showcases the MLP Draft Show. All that and more on Inside MLP!

Stay connected and never miss an update!
👉 Live scoring, player stats, and event info: https://www.majorleaguepickleball.co
👇 Join our community on social!
Instagram: instagram.com/MajorLea

videos.title: Inside MLP - 2026 Player Keeper Reactions
videos.description:
In this episode of Inside MLP, Dave Fleming and Will Daughton provide their initial reactions to the Player Keeper Deadline and discuss how some decisions could have massive implications for the upcoming Major League Pickleball draft on Feb. 27.

See the full list of Keepers by team here: https://majorleaguepickleball.co/news/major-league-pickleball-announces-2026-player-keepers/. 


Stay connecte

videos.title: What is Pickleball?
videos.description:
Pickleball is one of the fastest growing sports in the United States and for good reason. In this video, we break down what Pickleball is, why it’s so popular, and how you can get started playing.

Pickleball is a paddle sport that combines elements of tennis, table tennis, and badminton into one fun and accessible game. One of its biggest appeals is that players of all ages and skill levels can p

videos.title: Pickleball Fundamentals For Beginners
videos.description:
Having a strong foundation in pickleball fundamentals will set you up for long-term success on the court. In this video, we break down some of the most important fundamentals every beginner should learn to start playing with confidence.

First, it all starts with your grip. We recommend the continental grip, which you can find by forming a “V” with your dominant hand and placing it along the edge

videos.title: Most Important Rules to Start Playing Pickleball
videos.description:
If you don’t know the rules, you can’t play! In this video, we break down the most important pickleball rules so you can step on the court with confidence and start playing right away.

First, understand the boundaries of the court. The baselines and sidelines mark the limits, and for a ball to be in, it must land on or within these lines. If any part of the ball touches the line, it’s considered

videos.title: Volley Fundamentals in Pickleball
videos.description:
The volley is one of the most important and versatile shots in pickleball. In this video, we break down volley fundamentals so you can improve your control, reaction time, and ability to win points at the net.

A volley is any shot hit out of the air before the ball bounces. These shots can be offensive or defensive, hard or soft, but if you’re making contact before it hits the ground, it’s a voll

items.title: Master the Fourth Shot in Pickleball to Win More Points
items.summary: The fourth shot in pickleball is your chance to seize control of the rally. By engaging your body and legs with a longer swing, you can generate maximum power and spin that forces errors from your opponents.

items.title: How to Hit a Pickleball Tweener Without Falling Over
items.summary: A pickleball tweener is a last-resort shot used when a lob gets behind you. Let the ball drop to knee height, swing upward between your legs, and keep moving for balance. Focus on getting it back in play—usually as a soft lob—rather than hitting a winner. Let’s clear this up righ

items.summary: Most intermediate rec players do not lose attacking points because they are afraid to be aggressive. They lose them because they attack the wrong ball with the wrong swing. They try to roll a ball that is already high enough to punish. Or they try to smack a ball that is too low

items.title: Pickleball Butt: What It Is, Why It Happens, and How to Get Back on Court Safely
items.summary: “Pickleball butt” usually refers to deep buttock or hip pain from piriformis irritation, sciatic nerve irritation, high hamstring issues, or hip overload. Rest, modify play, strengthen hips/glutes gradually, and get checked if pain shoots down the leg, causes weakness, limping, o
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Two-Handed Backhand Speed-Up That Catches Everyone Off Guard
items.summary: The two-handed backhand down-the-line speed-up is a doubles attack used when an opponent cheats toward the middle. Stay low, contact the ball in front, use a compact swing, aim inside the sideline, and hit with controlled pace and topspin rather than full power. A two-handed back
items.author: Ana

items.title: Are You Taking Your Pickleball Dinks Too Early or Too Late?
items.summary: For most pickleball dinks, the best contact point is near the top of the bounce, when the ball is high enough to guide cleanly but no longer springing upward. Letting it fall lower can help if you are late or stretched, but waiting too long often makes the dink harder to control.
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Treat and Prevent Sore Legs in Pickleball
items.summary: Sore legs from pickleball are usually caused by all the stopping, shuffling, lunging, and low positioning the sport demands, not just by how far you move. Mild soreness is common, but sharp pain, limping, swelling, or weakness are signs you should back off and take it more seriou
items.author: Ana

items.title: Should You Grunt in Pickleball? A Real Guide for Rec Players
items.summary: Grunting in pickleball is not automatically wrong, but loud, constant grunting can become distracting. A controlled exhale may help rhythm, looseness, and power, but rec players should keep it proportional. If the sound shows up on every dink, reset, and routine volley, it is pro
items.author: Ana
